text.title: Message Queue Reliability Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real mq-reliability deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Broker advertises 'message published' before it is written to a durable queue; broker crash drops messages.
antipattern.root-cause: Publisher confirms disabled; default fire-and-forget assumed reliable.
antipattern.fix: Enable publisher confirms (RabbitMQ) / acks=all (Kafka); treat unconfirmed messages as failed.

antipattern.symptom: DLQ exists but no alert; messages accumulate; business operation breaks silently for hours.
antipattern.fix: Alert on DLQ depth > 0 sustained 5 minutes; replay runbook on hand.

antipattern.symptom: One slow consumer gobbles the entire queue; sibling consumers starve.
antipattern.root-cause: Prefetch_count not configured; default is unbounded.
antipattern.fix: Set prefetch_count (10 as starting point); tune by processing time + consumer count.

antipattern.symptom: Broker hiccup → all consumers retry simultaneously → thundering herd extends the outage.
antipattern.fix: Exponential backoff + 20-50% jitter; hard ceiling on retries before DLQ.

antipattern.symptom: Producer renames a field; consumer deserialization fails silently; messages flow to DLQ.
antipattern.root-cause: Schemas live in code, not a registry; no compatibility check at publish time.
antipattern.fix: Register every schema; producer publish checks compat; consumer pins schema version.
